unit.comment: /*************************************************************************** * _ _ ____ _ * Project ___| | | | _ \| | * / __| | | | |_) | | * | (__| |_| | _ <| |___ * \___|\___/|_| \_\_____| * * Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al. * * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html. * * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file. * * This software is distributed on an "AS IS" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY * KIND, either express or implied. * * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl * ***************************************************************************/
/* use our own printf() functions */
/* keep this as LAST include */

function.name: new_getout

unit.comment:
/* memcrlf() has two modes. Both operate on a given memory area with a specified size.
countcrlf FALSE - return number of bytes from the start that DO NOT include any CR or LF or NULL
countcrlf TRUE - return number of bytes from the start that are ONLY CR or LF or NULL.
*/

function.name: memcrlf
function.parameter_list: (char *orig, bool countcrlf, /* TRUE if we count CRLF, FALSE if we count non-CRLF */ size_t max)

unit.comment: /* big enough ? */

function.name: file2string
file2memory

unit.comment: /* * Parse the string and write the long in the given address. Return PARAM_OK * on success, otherwise a parameter specific error enum. * * Since this function gets called with the 'nextarg' pointer from within the * getparameter a lot, we must check it for NULL before accessing the str * data. */

function.name: getnum
str2num
oct2nummax

unit.comment: /* * Parse the string and write the long in the given address. Return PARAM_OK * on success, otherwise a parameter error enum. ONLY ACCEPTS POSITIVE NUMBERS! * * Since this function gets called with the 'nextarg' pointer from within the * getparameter a lot, we must check it for NULL before accessing the str * data. */

function.name: str2unum

unit.comment: /* * Parse the string and write the long in the given address if it is below the * maximum allowed value. Return PARAM_OK on success, otherwise a parameter * error enum. ONLY ACCEPTS POSITIVE NUMBERS! * * Since this function gets called with the 'nextarg' pointer from within the * getparameter a lot, we must check it for NULL before accessing the str * data. */

function.name: str2unummax

unit.comment: /* * Parse the string and write the double in the given address. Return PARAM_OK * on success, otherwise a parameter specific error enum. * * The 'max' argument is the maximum value allowed, as the numbers are often * multiplied when later used. * * Since this function gets called with the 'nextarg' pointer from within the * getparameter a lot, we must check it for NULL before accessing the str * data. */

function.name: str2double

unit.comment: /* * Parse the string as seconds with decimals, and write the number of * milliseconds that corresponds in the given address. Return PARAM_OK on * success, otherwise a parameter error enum. ONLY ACCEPTS POSITIVE NUMBERS! * * The 'max' argument is the maximum value allowed, as the numbers are often * multiplied when later used. * * Since this function gets called with the 'nextarg' pointer from within the * getparameter a lot, we must check it for NULL before accessing the str * data. */

function.name: secs2ms

unit.comment: /* Return index of prototype token in set, card(set) if not found. Can be called with proto == NULL to get card(set). */

function.name: protoset_index

unit.comment: /* Include protocol token in set. */

function.name: protoset_set

unit.comment: /* Exclude protocol token from set. */

function.name: protoset_clear

unit.comment: /* * Parse the string and provide an allocated libcurl compatible protocol * string output. Return non-zero on failure, zero on success. * * The string is a list of protocols * * Since this function gets called with the 'nextarg' pointer from within the * getparameter a lot, we must check it for NULL before accessing the str * data. */
/* Enough room for 64 10-chars proto names. */

function.name: proto2num
function.parameter_list: (struct OperationConfig *config, const char * const *val, char **ostr, const char *str)

unit.comment: /** * Check if the given string is a protocol supported by libcurl * * @param str the protocol name * @return PARAM_OK protocol supported * @return PARAM_LIBCURL_UNSUPPORTED_PROTOCOL protocol not supported * @return PARAM_REQUIRES_PARAMETER missing parameter */

function.name: check_protocol

unit.comment: /** * Parses the given string looking for an offset (which may be a * larger-than-integer value). The offset CANNOT be negative! * * @param val the offset to populate * @param str the buffer containing the offset * @return PARAM_OK if successful, a parameter specific error enum if failure. */

function.name: str2offset
checkpasswd
function.parameter_list: (const char *kind, /* for what purpose */ const size_t i, /* operation index */ const bool last, /* TRUE if last operation */ char **userpwd)

function.name: add2list
ftpfilemethod
ftpcccmethod
delegation
my_useragent

unit.comment: /* * inlist() returns true if the given 'checkfor' header is present in the * header list. */

function.name: inlist
get_args

unit.comment: /* * Parse the string and modify ssl_version in the val argument. Return PARAM_OK * on success, otherwise a parameter error enum. ONLY ACCEPTS POSITIVE NUMBERS! * * Since this function gets called with the 'nextarg' pointer from within the * getparameter a lot, we must check it for NULL before accessing the str * data. */

function.name: str2tls_max
